findings.name: Form submission without CSRF token validation
findings.description: A form processing handler does not validate a CSRF token, allowing cross-site request forgery attacks.
findings.remediation: Include a CSRF token in all POST forms and validate it server-side. OJS provides CSRFTokenManager for this purpose.

findings.name: XML entity loading not disabled
findings.description: XML parsing code does not call libxml_disable_entity_loader() or use LIBXML_NOENT flag, leaving it vulnerable to XXE.
findings.remediation: Always disable external entities before XML parsing. Use LIBXML_NOENT and LIBXML_NONET flags.

findings.name: Hook modifying security-critical data
findings.description: A hook handler modifies authentication or authorization data without proper validation.
findings.remediation: Audit all hooks that modify security-critical data. Ensure proper validation and logging.

findings.name: Form submission without CSRF token validation
findings.description: A form processing handler does not validate a CSRF token, allowing cross-site request forgery attacks.
findings.remediation: Include a CSRF token in all POST forms and validate it server-side. OJS provides CSRFTokenManager for this purpose.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: Form submission without CSRF token validation
findings.description: A form processing handler does not validate a CSRF token, allowing cross-site request forgery attacks.
findings.remediation: Include a CSRF token in all POST forms and validate it server-side. OJS provides CSRFTokenManager for this purpose.

findings.name: OJS handler missing CSRF check
findings.description: An OJS handler method that modifies data does not call the CSRF validation method before processing.
findings.remediation: Add $this->validate() or CSRF token checking in all handler methods that modify data.

findings.name: SQL Injection via tainted input
findings.description:
Data from user input (array(
					'name' => $fileManager->getUploadedFileName($settingName),
					'uploadName' => $uploadName,
					'dateUploaded' => Core::getCurrentDate()
				)) flows into a dangerous function (updateSetting) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries. If using OJS DAO, use $this->_driver->escapeString() or intval() for integer parameters.

findings.name: OJS handler missing CSRF check
findings.description: An OJS handler method that modifies data does not call the CSRF validation method before processing.
findings.remediation: Add $this->validate() or CSRF token checking in all handler methods that modify data.

findings.name: HTTP Header Injection via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (call_user_func(array($this->cache_class, 'create'), $this->cache_location, $favicon_filename, 'spi')) flows into a dangerous function (isset) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Validate and sanitize header values. Remove newline characters (\r\n) from user input before passing to header() or setcookie().

findings.name: Server-Side Request Forgery via tainted input

findings.remediation: Validate and whitelist allowed URLs/hosts. Block internal/private IP ranges. Use a URL parser to enforce allowed schemes (http/https only).

findings.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ($file->body) flows into a dangerous function (mysql_fetch_row) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Apply htmlspecialchars($var, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8') before output. In OJS Smarty templates, use |escape modifier. For rich text, use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml().

findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions

findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: SQL Injection via string concatenation in query
findings.description: A SQL query is built using string concatenation with variables that may contain user input, creating a SQL injection vulnerability.
findings.remediation: Never concatenate variables into SQL strings. Use parameterized queries or the OJS DAO pattern with proper escaping.

findings.name: Insecure deserialization with user input
findings.description: unserialize() is called with data that may originate from user input, allowing object injection attacks.
findings.remediation: Avoid unserialize() with user input. Use json_decode() instead. If unserialize is required, use allowed_classes option: unserialize($data, ['allowed_classes' => false])

findings.name: Path traversal in file operations
findings.description: User input containing path traversal sequences (../) is used in file operations, allowing access to arbitrary files.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components from user input. Validate with realpath() and check the result is within allowed directories.

findings.description: OJS versions before 3.2.0-4 contain SSRF vulnerabilities that allow authenticated users to make requests to internal services.
findings.remediation: Update to OJS 3.2.0-4 or later. Validate and whitelist allowed URLs. Block internal/private IP ranges.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: Plugin loading without validation
findings.description: OJS plugin loading does not validate the plugin source or integrity, allowing potentially malicious plugins to be loaded.
findings.remediation: Validate plugin integrity using checksums before loading. Only load plugins from the official OJS plugin gallery.

findings.description: Data from user input ($request->getUserVar('orderBy')) flows into a dangerous function (assign) without adequate sanitization.

findings.name: CVE-2021-27231 - Path traversal
findings.description: OJS versions before 3.2.1-5 are vulnerable to path traversal allowing unauthenticated access to arbitrary files on the server.
findings.remediation: Update to OJS 3.2.1-5 or later. Validate all file paths with basename() and realpath().

findings.remediation: Apply htmlspecialchars($var, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8') before output. In OJS Smarty templates, use |escape modifier. For rich text, use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml().

findings.name: Stored XSS via Smarty template assign without escaping
findings.description: The OJS $templateMgr->assign() method passes unsanitized data to a Smarty template, which may render it without escaping.
findings.remediation: Sanitize data before assigning to templates. Use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml() for rich text or htmlspecialchars() for plain text.

findings.name: Stored XSS via Smarty template assign without escaping

findings.remediation: Sanitize data before assigning to templates. Use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml() for rich text or htmlspecialchars() for plain text.

findings.name: OJS handler missing CSRF check
findings.description: An OJS handler method that modifies data does not call the CSRF validation method before processing.
findings.remediation: Add $this->validate() or CSRF token checking in all handler methods that modify data.

findings.name: Remote Code Execution via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (' ' . escapeshellarg($file)) flows into a dangerous function (passthru) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.remediation: Include a CSRF token in all POST forms and validate it server-side. OJS provides CSRFTokenManager for this purpose.

findings.name: SQL Injection via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (Request::getUserVar('changes')) flows into a dangerous function (update) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries. If using OJS DAO, use $this->_driver->escapeString() or intval() for integer parameters.

findings.name: HTTP Header Injection via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (Request::getUserVar('stack')) flows into a dangerous function (header) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Validate and sanitize header values. Remove newline characters (\r\n) from user input before passing to header() or setcookie().

findings.name: Path Traversal / File Inclusion via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (Request::getUserVar('stack')) flows into a dangerous function (readfile) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components. Validate paths with realpath() and ensure they're within the expected directory. Never use user input in include/require.

findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components from user input. Validate with realpath() and check the result is within allowed directories.

findings.name: XML entity loading not disabled
findings.description: XML parsing code does not call libxml_disable_entity_loader() or use LIBXML_NOENT flag, leaving it vulnerable to XXE.
findings.remediation: Always disable external entities before XML parsing. Use LIBXML_NOENT and LIBXML_NONET flags.

findings.name: XML entity loading not disabled
findings.description: XML parsing code does not call libxml_disable_entity_loader() or use LIBXML_NOENT flag, leaving it vulnerable to XXE.
findings.remediation: Always disable external entities before XML parsing. Use LIBXML_NOENT and LIBXML_NONET flags.

findings.remediation: Update to OJS 3.2.1-5 or later. Validate all file paths with basename() and realpath().

findings.name: Remote Code Execution via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (dirname($_SERVER['SCRIPT_FILENAME']) . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR . $this->getPluginPath() . '/transform/test.xml') flows into a dangerous function (trim) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: Remote Code Execution via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (dirname($_SERVER['SCRIPT_FILENAME']) . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR . $this->getPluginPath() . '/transform/test.xml') flows into a dangerous function (preg_replace) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: SQL Injection via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ($fileManager->getUploadedFileName('customXSL')) flows into a dangerous function (updateSetting) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries. If using OJS DAO, use $this->_driver->escapeString() or intval() for integer parameters.

findings.name: CVE-2023-33970 - XSS via galley label
findings.description: OJS versions before 3.3.0-16 are vulnerable to stored XSS through the galley label field. An authenticated user can inject JavaScript via a crafted galley label.
findings.remediation: Update to OJS 3.3.0-16 or later. Apply htmlspecialchars() to galley label output.

findings.name: Form submission without CSRF token validation
findings.description: A form processing handler does not validate a CSRF token, allowing cross-site request forgery attacks.
findings.remediation: Include a CSRF token in all POST forms and validate it server-side. OJS provides CSRFTokenManager for this purpose.

findings.name: OJS handler missing CSRF check
findings.description: An OJS handler method that modifies data does not call the CSRF validation method before processing.
findings.remediation: Add $this->validate() or CSRF token checking in all handler methods that modify data.

653 | 		$curl = curl_init();

>>>  655 | 		curl_setopt_array($curl, array(
     656 | 			CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER => true,
     657 | 			CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER => $headers,
findings.remediation: Validate and sanitize header values. Remove newline characters (\r\n) from user input before passing to header() or setcookie().

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: SQL Injection via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (array('originalFileName' => $publicFileManager->truncateFileName($originalFileName, 127), 'fileName' => $newFileName, 'width' => $width, 'height' => $height, 'altText' => $coverPageAltText)) flows into a dangerous function (updateSetting) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries. If using OJS DAO, use $this->_driver->escapeString() or intval() for integer parameters.

findings.name: Form submission without CSRF token validation
findings.description: A form processing handler does not validate a CSRF token, allowing cross-site request forgery attacks.
findings.remediation: Include a CSRF token in all POST forms and validate it server-side. OJS provides CSRFTokenManager for this purpose.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: Debug function left in production code

findings.remediation: Remove all debug functions from production code. Use proper logging instead of debug output functions.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.

findings.name: Path traversal in file operations
findings.description: User input containing path traversal sequences (../) is used in file operations, allowing access to arbitrary files.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components from user input. Validate with realpath() and check the result is within allowed directories.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: CVE-2020-28113 - Server-Side Request Forgery
findings.description: OJS versions before 3.2.0-4 contain SSRF vulnerabilities that allow authenticated users to make requests to internal services.
findings.remediation: Update to OJS 3.2.0-4 or later. Validate and whitelist allowed URLs. Block internal/private IP ranges.

findings.name: Remote Code Execution via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ($request->getUserVar('target')) flows into a dangerous function (assert) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: Remote Code Execution via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (array($objectTypes[$target] => (array) $request->getUserVar($target . 'Id'))) flows into a dangerous function (assert) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: HTTP Header Injection via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (array($objectTypes[$target] => (array) $request->getUserVar($target . 'Id'))) flows into a dangerous function (header) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Validate and sanitize header values. Remove newline characters (\r\n) from user input before passing to header() or setcookie().

findings.name: Path Traversal / File Inclusion via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (array($objectTypes[$target] => (array) $request->getUserVar($target . 'Id'))) flows into a dangerous function (readfile) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components. Validate paths with realpath() and ensure they're within the expected directory. Never use user input in include/require.

findings.name: Path Traversal / File Inclusion via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (array($objectTypes[$target] => (array) $request->getUserVar($target . 'Id'))) flows into a dangerous function (copyFile) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components. Validate paths with realpath() and ensure they're within the expected directory. Never use user input in include/require.

findings.name: Remote Code Execution via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (dirname($tempfile) . '/') flows into a dangerous function (assert) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: Stored XSS via Smarty template assign without escaping
findings.description: The OJS $templateMgr->assign() method passes unsanitized data to a Smarty template, which may render it without escaping.
findings.remediation: Sanitize data before assigning to templates. Use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml() for rich text or htmlspecialchars() for plain text.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: Unsafe hook callback execution
findings.description: A hook callback uses call_user_func or similar with potentially user-controlled callback names.
findings.remediation: Validate callback functions before execution. Use typed callable parameters.

findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: Form submission without CSRF token validation
findings.description: A form processing handler does not validate a CSRF token, allowing cross-site request forgery attacks.
findings.remediation: Include a CSRF token in all POST forms and validate it server-side. OJS provides CSRFTokenManager for this purpose.

findings.name: Remote Code Execution via tainted input
findings.description:
Data from user input ("mEDRA: 200 - uploaded file is not valid cvc-complex-type.2.4.a: ".
			"Invalid content was found starting with element 'UnknownElement'. " .
			"One of '{\"http://www.editeur.org/onix/DOIMetadata/2.0\":SerialVersion}' is expected.") flows into a dangerous function (assertEquals) without adequate sanitization.

taint_path.source:
"mEDRA: 200 - uploaded file is not valid cvc-complex-type.2.4.a: ".
			"Invalid content was found starting with element 'UnknownElement'. " .
			"One of '{\"http://www.editeur.org/onix/DOIMetadata/2.0\":SerialVersion}' is expected."
intermediate_steps:
$expectedError = "mEDRA: 200 - uploaded file is not valid cvc-complex-type.2.4.a: ".
			"Invalid content was found starting with element 'UnknownElement'. " .
			"One of '{\"http://www.editeur.org/onix/DOIMetadata/2.0\":SerialVersion}' is expected." (line 49)

findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: XML entity loading not disabled
findings.description: XML parsing code does not call libxml_disable_entity_loader() or use LIBXML_NOENT flag, leaving it vulnerable to XXE.
findings.remediation: Always disable external entities before XML parsing. Use LIBXML_NOENT and LIBXML_NONET flags.

findings.name: Path traversal in file operations
findings.description: User input containing path traversal sequences (../) is used in file operations, allowing access to arbitrary files.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components from user input. Validate with realpath() and check the result is within allowed directories.

findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: Path traversal in file operations
findings.description: User input containing path traversal sequences (../) is used in file operations, allowing access to arbitrary files.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components from user input. Validate with realpath() and check the result is within allowed directories.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: CVE-2020-28113 - Server-Side Request Forgery
findings.description: OJS versions before 3.2.0-4 contain SSRF vulnerabilities that allow authenticated users to make requests to internal services.
findings.remediation: Update to OJS 3.2.0-4 or later. Validate and whitelist allowed URLs. Block internal/private IP ranges.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: Hardcoded authentication credentials
findings.description: Authentication credentials are hardcoded in the source code, which could be exposed through source code leaks.
findings.remediation: Store credentials in environment variables or a secure configuration file outside the web root. Never hardcode secrets in source code.

findings.name: MD5 used for security-sensitive operation
findings.description: md5() is used for generating tokens, signatures, or other security-sensitive values where collision resistance matters.
findings.remediation: Use hash('sha256', ...) or hash_hmac() for security operations. For tokens, use random_bytes() or bin2hex(random_bytes(32)).

findings.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (Request::getUserVar('subscriptionCreated') == 1 ? 1 : 0) flows into a dangerous function (assign) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Apply htmlspecialchars($var, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8') before output. In OJS Smarty templates, use |escape modifier. For rich text, use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml().

findings.name: Hardcoded authentication credentials
findings.description: Authentication credentials are hardcoded in the source code, which could be exposed through source code leaks.
findings.remediation: Store credentials in environment variables or a secure configuration file outside the web root. Never hardcode secrets in source code.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: SQL Injection via tainted input
findings.description:
Data from user input ('SELECT	a.article_id,
				af.date_uploaded AS date_rv_uploaded,
				r.review_id,
				u.date_registered,
				r.reviewer_id,
				r.quality,
				r.date_assigned,
				r.date_completed
			FROM	articles a,
				article_files af,
				review_assignments r
				LEFT JOIN users u ON (u.user_id = r.reviewer_id)
			WHERE	a.journal_id = ?
				AND r.submission_id = a.article_id
				AND af.article_id = a.article_id
				AND af.file_id = a.review_file_id
				AND af.revision = 1' .
			($dateStart !== null ? ' AND a.date_submitted >= ' . $this->datetimeToDB($dateStart) : '') .
			($dateEnd !== null ? ' AND a.date_submitted <= ' . $this->datetimeToDB($dateEnd) : '') .
			$sectionSql) flows into a dangerous function (retrieve) without adequate sanitization.

taint_path.source:
'SELECT	a.article_id,
				af.date_uploaded AS date_rv_uploaded,
				r.review_id,
				u.date_registered,
				r.reviewer_id,
				r.quality,
				r.date_assigned,
				r.date_completed
			FROM	articles a,
				article_files af,
				review_assignments r
				LEFT JOIN users u ON (u.user_id = r.reviewer_id)
			WHERE	a.journal_id = ?
				AND r.submission_id = a.article_id
				AND af.article_id = a.article_id
				AND af.file_id = a.review_file_id
				AND af.revision = 1' .
			($dateStart !== null ? ' AND a.date_submitted >= ' . $this->datetimeToDB($dateStart) : '') .
			($dateEnd !== null ? ' AND a.date_submitted <= ' . $this->datetimeToDB($dateEnd) : '') .
			$sectionSql
intermediate_steps:
$sql = 'SELECT	a.article_id,
				af.date_uploaded AS date_rv_uploaded,
				r.review_id,
				u.date_registered,
				r.reviewer_id,
				r.quality,
				r.date_assigned,
				r.date_completed
			FROM	articles a,
				article_files af,
				review_assignments r
				LEFT JOIN users u ON (u.user_id = r.reviewer_id)
			WHERE	a.journal_id = ?
				AND r.submission_id = a.article_id
				AND af.article_id = a.article_id
				AND af.file_id = a.review_file_id
				AND af.revision = 1' .
			($dateStart !== null ? ' AND a.date_submitted >= ' . $this->datetimeToDB($dateStart) : '') .
			($dateEnd !== null ? ' AND a.date_submitted <= ' . $this->datetimeToDB($dateEnd) : '') .
			$sectionSql (line 328)

findings.remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries. If using OJS DAO, use $this->_driver->escapeString() or intval() for integer parameters.

findings.name: Insecure Deserialization via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input (file_get_contents($filename)) flows into a dangerous function (unserialize) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Avoid unserialize() with user input. Use json_decode() instead. If unserialize is required, use allowed_classes option (PHP 7+).

findings.name: Insecure deserialization with user input
findings.description: unserialize() is called with data that may originate from user input, allowing object injection attacks.
findings.remediation: Avoid unserialize() with user input. Use json_decode() instead. If unserialize is required, use allowed_classes option: unserialize($data, ['allowed_classes' => false])

findings.name: Path traversal in file operations
findings.description: User input containing path traversal sequences (../) is used in file operations, allowing access to arbitrary files.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components from user input. Validate with realpath() and check the result is within allowed directories.

findings.name: Path traversal in file operations
findings.description: User input containing path traversal sequences (../) is used in file operations, allowing access to arbitrary files.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components from user input. Validate with realpath() and check the result is within allowed directories.

findings.name: Debug function left in production code
findings.description: Debug functions (var_dump, print_r, debug_backtrace) are present in production code, potentially leaking sensitive information.
findings.remediation: Remove all debug functions from production code. Use proper logging instead of debug output functions.

findings.name: CVE-2023-33970 - XSS via galley label
findings.description: OJS versions before 3.3.0-16 are vulnerable to stored XSS through the galley label field. An authenticated user can inject JavaScript via a crafted galley label.
findings.remediation: Update to OJS 3.3.0-16 or later. Apply htmlspecialchars() to galley label output.

taint_path.source:
'SELECT DISTINCT
				a.*,
				scf.date_completed as copyedit_completed,
				spr.date_completed as proofread_completed,
				sle.date_completed as layout_completed,
				SUBSTRING(COALESCE(actl.setting_value, actpl.setting_value) FROM 1 FOR 255) AS submission_clean_title,
				aap.last_name AS author_name,
				e.can_review AS can_review,
				e.can_edit AS can_edit,
				SUBSTRING(COALESCE(stl.setting_value, stpl.setting_value) FROM 1 FOR 255) AS section_title,
				SUBSTRING(COALESCE(sal.setting_value, sapl.setting_value) FROM 1 FOR 255) AS section_abbrev,
				r2.review_revision
			FROM	articles a
				LEFT JOIN authors aa ON (aa.submission_id = a.article_id)
				LEFT JOIN authors aap ON (aap.submission_id = a.article_id AND aap.primary_contact = 1)
				LEFT JOIN edit_assignments e ON (e.article_id = a.article_id)
				LEFT JOIN users ed ON (e.editor_id = ed.user_id)
				LEFT JOIN sections s ON (s.section_id = a.section_id)
				LEFT JOIN signoffs scf ON (a.article_id = scf.assoc_id AND scf.assoc_type = ? AND scf.symbolic = ?)
				LEFT JOIN users ce ON (scf.user_id = ce.user_id)
				LEFT JOIN signoffs spr ON (a.article_id = spr.assoc_id AND spr.assoc_type = ? AND spr.symbolic = ?)
				LEFT JOIN users pe ON (pe.user_id = spr.user_id)
				LEFT JOIN review_rounds r2 ON (a.article_id = r2.submission_id and a.current_round = r2.round)
				LEFT JOIN signoffs sle ON (a.article_id = sle.assoc_id AND sle.assoc_type = ? AND sle.symbolic = ?) LEFT JOIN users le ON (le.user_id = sle.user_id)
				LEFT JOIN section_settings stpl ON (s.section_id = stpl.section_id AND stpl.setting_name = ? AND stpl.locale = ?)
				LEFT JOIN section_settings stl ON (s.section_id = stl.section_id AND stl.setting_name = ? AND stl.locale = ?)
				LEFT JOIN section_settings sapl ON (s.section_id = sapl.section_id AND sapl.setting_name = ? AND sapl.locale = ?)
				LEFT JOIN section_settings sal ON (s.section_id = sal.section_id AND sal.setting_name = ? AND sal.locale = ?)
				LEFT JOIN article_settings actpl ON (actpl.article_id = a.article_id AND actpl.setting_name = ? AND actpl.locale = a.locale)
				LEFT JOIN article_settings actl ON (a.article_id = actl.article_id AND actl.setting_name = ? AND actl.locale = ?)
				LEFT JOIN article_settings atpl ON (atpl.article_id = a.article_id AND atpl.setting_name = ? AND atpl.locale = a.locale)
				LEFT JOIN article_settings atl ON (a.article_id = atl.article_id AND atl.setting_name = ? AND atl.locale = ?)
				LEFT JOIN edit_decisions edec ON (a.article_id = edec.article_id)
				LEFT JOIN edit_decisions edec2 ON (a.article_id = edec2.article_id AND edec.edit_decision_id < edec2.edit_decision_id)
			WHERE	a.journal_id = ?
				AND e.editor_id = ?
				AND a.submission_progress = 0' . (!empty($additionalWhereSql)?" AND ($additionalWhereSql)":'') . '
				AND edec2.edit_decision_id IS NULL'

findings.name: Unsalted hash usage
findings.description: A hash function is used without a salt, making it vulnerable to rainbow table attacks.
findings.remediation: Use password_hash() which automatically handles salting. If using hash(), prepend a unique per-user salt.

findings.name: Weak token generation for password reset
findings.description: Password reset tokens are generated using weak randomness (rand(), mt_rand(), uniqid()) instead of cryptographically secure methods.
findings.remediation: Use random_bytes() or openssl_random_pseudo_bytes() for generating security tokens. In OJS, use PKPSecurity::generateRandomToken().

findings.name: OJS handler missing CSRF check
findings.description: An OJS handler method that modifies data does not call the CSRF validation method before processing.
findings.code_snippet:
93 | 	 * @param $role Role
      94 | 	 */
>>>   95 | 	function deleteRole(&$role) {
      96 | 		return $this->update(
      97 | 			'DELETE FROM roles WHERE journal_id = ? AND user_id = ? AND role_id = ?',
findings.remediation: Add $this->validate() or CSRF token checking in all handler methods that modify data.

findings.name: OJS handler missing CSRF check

findings.remediation: Add $this->validate() or CSRF token checking in all handler methods that modify data.

findings.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ($request->getUserVar('articleId')) flows into a dangerous function (displayEditForm) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Apply htmlspecialchars($var, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8') before output. In OJS Smarty templates, use |escape modifier. For rich text, use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml().

findings.name: Stored XSS via Smarty template assign without escaping
findings.description: The OJS $templateMgr->assign() method passes unsanitized data to a Smarty template, which may render it without escaping.
findings.remediation: Sanitize data before assigning to templates. Use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml() for rich text or htmlspecialchars() for plain text.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: CVE-2021-27231 - Path traversal
findings.description: OJS versions before 3.2.1-5 are vulnerable to path traversal allowing unauthenticated access to arbitrary files on the server.
findings.remediation: Update to OJS 3.2.1-5 or later. Validate all file paths with basename() and realpath().

findings.name: File upload using client-supplied filename
findings.description: The original filename from the upload is used directly, which could contain path traversal or dangerous extensions.
findings.remediation: Generate a random filename server-side. If the original name is needed, sanitize it with basename() and validate the extension.

findings.name: CVE-2022-24822 - RCE via file upload
findings.description: OJS versions before 3.3.0-10 allow remote code execution through specially crafted file uploads that bypass extension validation in the submission process.
findings.remediation: Update to OJS 3.3.0-10 or later. Implement strict file extension whitelisting and magic byte validation.

findings.name: OJS handler missing CSRF check
findings.description: An OJS handler method that modifies data does not call the CSRF validation method before processing.
findings.remediation: Add $this->validate() or CSRF token checking in all handler methods that modify data.

findings.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ((int) $request->getUserVar('statisticsYear')) flows into a dangerous function (assign) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Apply htmlspecialchars($var, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8') before output. In OJS Smarty templates, use |escape modifier. For rich text, use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml().

findings.name: Insecure Deserialization via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ($request->getUserVar('orderBy')) flows into a dangerous function (unserialize) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Avoid unserialize() with user input. Use json_decode() instead. If unserialize is required, use allowed_classes option (PHP 7+).

findings.name: Insecure deserialization with user input
findings.description: unserialize() is called with data that may originate from user input, allowing object injection attacks.
findings.remediation: Avoid unserialize() with user input. Use json_decode() instead. If unserialize is required, use allowed_classes option: unserialize($data, ['allowed_classes' => false])

findings.name: Insecure Direct Object Reference - missing authorization check
findings.description: Database object is accessed using a user-supplied ID without checking if the current user has permission to access it.
findings.remediation: Always validate that the current user has access rights to the requested resource. Use OJS role-based access control (RBAC).

findings.name: phpinfo() exposed
findings.description: phpinfo() is called in the codebase, potentially exposing server configuration details to attackers.
findings.remediation: Remove phpinfo() calls from production code entirely.

findings.name: phpinfo() exposed
findings.description: phpinfo() is called in the codebase, potentially exposing server configuration details to attackers.
findings.remediation: Remove phpinfo() calls from production code entirely.

findings.name: SQL Injection via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ($request->getUserVar('categoriesEnabled')==1?true:false) flows into a dangerous function (updateSetting) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Use prepared statements or parameterized queries. If using OJS DAO, use $this->_driver->escapeString() or intval() for integer parameters.

findings.name: Stored XSS via Smarty template assign without escaping
findings.description: The OJS $templateMgr->assign() method passes unsanitized data to a Smarty template, which may render it without escaping.
findings.remediation: Sanitize data before assigning to templates. Use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml() for rich text or htmlspecialchars() for plain text.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: MIME type check bypass
findings.description: File type validation relies only on the Content-Type header, which can be easily spoofed by attackers.
findings.remediation: Use finfo_file() with FILEINFO_MIME_TYPE to detect the actual file type from magic bytes, not the client-supplied Content-Type.

findings.name: Stored XSS via Smarty template assign without escaping
findings.description: The OJS $templateMgr->assign() method passes unsanitized data to a Smarty template, which may render it without escaping.
findings.remediation: Sanitize data before assigning to templates. Use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml() for rich text or htmlspecialchars() for plain text.

findings.name: OJS handler missing CSRF check
findings.description: An OJS handler method that modifies data does not call the CSRF validation method before processing.
findings.remediation: Add $this->validate() or CSRF token checking in all handler methods that modify data.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: Path traversal in file operations
findings.description: User input containing path traversal sequences (../) is used in file operations, allowing access to arbitrary files.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components from user input. Validate with realpath() and check the result is within allowed directories.

findings.name: Path traversal in file operations

findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components from user input. Validate with realpath() and check the result is within allowed directories.

findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.

findings.name: Path Traversal / File Inclusion via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ($tmpDir . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR . $fileName) flows into a dangerous function (copyFile) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components. Validate paths with realpath() and ensure they're within the expected directory. Never use user input in include/require.

findings.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ($tmpDir . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR . $fileName) flows into a dangerous function (printf) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Apply htmlspecialchars($var, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8') before output. In OJS Smarty templates, use |escape modifier. For rich text, use PKPString::stripUnsafeHtml().

findings.remediation: Use basename() to strip directory components. Validate paths with realpath() and ensure they're within the expected directory. Never use user input in include/require.

findings.name: Remote Code Execution via tainted input
findings.description: Data from user input ($tmpDir . DIRECTORY_SEPARATOR . $fileName) flows into a dangerous function (exec) without adequate sanitization.
findings.remediation: Avoid using exec(), system(), or eval() with user input. Use escapeshellarg() and escapeshellcmd() if shell execution is absolutely necessary.

findings.name: Command Injection via shell execution functions
findings.description: Shell execution functions (exec, system, shell_exec, passthru, popen) receive user-controlled input, enabling OS command injection.
findings.remediation: Avoid calling shell commands with user input. If necessary, use escapeshellarg() for each argument and escapeshellcmd() for the command.
